problem_statement: The model cannot generate useful pantry-aware recipes unless the server assembles a compact and reliable representation of current stock, pantry risk signals, and user preferences. If this context is inconsistent or too noisy, the feature will produce generic recipes that ignore the pantry the user actually has.
in_scope: Add a server-side context builder that loads pantry item names quantities units locations and expiration dates for the signed-in user
Include relevant user settings such as dietary preferences and default kitchen conventions when available
Define a normalized payload for AI recipe generation that separates in-stock ingredients from advisory metadata such as expiring soon items
out_of_scope: Rendering AI recipes in the UI
requirements: The context builder must only read data for the authenticated user
The context payload must include enough pantry detail to distinguish on-hand ingredients from expired or nearly expired items
The payload shape must be serializable and reusable by server actions or route handlers without UI coupling
acceptance_criteria: A server-side helper returns a normalized pantry AI context for the current user
The returned context includes pantry items user dietary preferences and a derived expiring-soon slice when relevant
The helper can be consumed by later AI generation code without additional DB reads in the UI layer
risks: Sending too much pantry detail will increase token cost and push the model toward noisy suggestions
Loose normalization may make duplicate pantry names look like separate ingredients to the model
implementation_notes: Start with server helpers near the recipe or AI action layer. Reuse pantry rows already modeled in src/db/schema.ts and bias toward a compact prompt payload rather than raw DB records.
